summary: Clear audience recording captures a fiery 1979 show highlighted by explosive “Eyes of the World”, soaring “Not Fade Away” with John Cipollina, and a scorching “Shakedown Street”.
review: The tape offers surprisingly clean auditorium ambience for an AUD source, letting the band’s high‑energy second set shine. Standout moments include a blistering “Estimated → Eyes of the World”, a monster “Not Fade Away” featuring Cipollina’s lead, and a raucous “Shakedown Street”. Overall the performance is tight, enthusiastic, and well‑captured despite occasional crowd boom.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (20+) give 4–5 stars and praise both the musical set and the clear audience recording; only a few low‑star or mixed comments appear.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear auditorium ambience, balanced mix with good instrument separation. Some listeners note heavy crowd boom and occasional synth interference, but overall the recording is listenable and captures the live energy.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid but a bit uneven; second set launches into a high‑octane jam marathon, with “Estimated” paving the way for an epic “Eyes of the World” and a monster “Not Fade Away”, delivering a satisfying arc that ends on a joyous “Around and Around”.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for scorching lead work (especially alongside John Cipollina), vocal power on several numbers, and a jazzy solo on “Candyman”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for strong bass foundation and more extroverted vocal contributions; his interplay with Brent’s keyboards highlighted in “Peggy‑O”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s first month shines – delicate keys on “Peggy‑O”, energetic organ/synth textures (though one reviewer felt a synth patch was slightly intrusive).
band_member_comments.Drums: Rhythm Devils’ generous drum segment praised; double‑drumming dynamics noted as a highlight of the era.
